title: Is Rei Lilith dead after EoE
display_title: Is Rei Lilith dead after EoE
wikitext:
[]: Is Rei/Lilith dead after EoE?             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Wed Jan 29, 2014 5:56 pm So, I was watching one of DouchebagChocolat's videos, and the clip of GNR/awakened Lilith decaying and falling apart played, which made me wonder: Is Lilith/Rei permanently dead after Shinji rejects Instrumentality? Let's break it down: >Bizarre rejection sequence + Shinji saying farewell to both Rei and his mummy. >Unit 01 bursts from Rei/Lilith's third eye. >A fountain of blood spurts from Rei/Lilith's neck, as she begins to rapidly decompose. >Instrumentality begins to grind to a halt, with the crosses and glowy shit disappearing. >Rei/Lilith continues to rot, all with a look of bliss on her face. >Her head finally separates from her neck, plummeting to the surface of the ruined Earth to lie amongst the seas of LCL. >Shinji emerges from LCL sea, completely loses his mind, Asuka "Kimochi Warui" ("I feel sick"/"How disgusting"/"Well this sucks") scene. >Fin. >Hideaki Anno's fits of trollish laughter (removed from the film after a test screening with it still in caused the audience's faces to burst like zits). OK, I made that last bit up. So, let's get the obvious out of the way: Rei/Lilith is biologically dead. Dead, dead, dead. With Instrumentality averted and her AT Field (or whatever) terminated, she could no longer substain her form and she collapses into chunks of Rei-sludge as a huge sack of meat the size of the moon tends to do when not supported by sci-fi magic. However, in Eva, death is...complicated. For all we know, Rei/Lilith's soul is very much intact and residing inside the body of a pink mountain goat. However (and bear in mind this is just my opinion) I think that Rei/Lilith's soul has permanently been destroyed, and even if not, isn't coming back any time soon. My evidence? Look at her face. She's not just unfazed by her disintegration, she's practically ecstatic. It's almost as if she wanted her head to fall off in a horrific literal tide of blood. And the scary thing is, it makes a lot of sense. Lilith/Rei has suffered more than just about anyone in Eva- because unlike the rest of the characters (who hardly have it great themselves) she's been putting up with it since the beginning of humanity. She's crashlanded on the wrong planet, been impaled upon the Lance of Longinus, unable to join her creations and in constant agony, had a huge amount of her children obliterated in Second Impact, been crucified and used as an LCL vending machine, had her very soul brutally vivisected, with most of it trapped inside a constantly decaying human body and the rest in a state of endless fury, imprisoned within the hulk of Unit 00, she's been used as a surrogate wife by a creepy beardy man, she's had to watch the one being she's been able to make an attachment to lose his mind- partially by her own hand, she's been vapourized and vomited back into an new body, she's been shown that she is utterly expendable, and then, to top it all off, she's been psuedo-raped by this beardy man (although at least she was united with her fellow Seed, Adam/Kaworu) and in the icing on the shit sundae, the gift to Shinji she thought she was giving (Instrumentality) completely tore apart what little sanity he had left. By this point, Lilith is probably furious, distraught, traumatized and in agony all at the same time. She wanted to die. She's finally being granted an end to her eternal torment, sweet oblivion, to allow her corpse to rest amongst the reborn forms of her children, and to finally be at peace. She has no reason to reincarnate herself, or even to keep her soul in existence (or at the very least out of eternal sleep). She is the tortured mother of mankind, and I don't think she fancies another season. Of course, this is just my theory. Any counter-theories, arguments, rants, tirades of abuse and death threats upon my plebeian self are welcomed. Thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Jan 29, 2014 6:27 pm Depends how you define 'dead'. I think she's stepped out of the linear experience of time and become a being coexistant with all places and times simultaneously. My evidence comes in the form of ep1 phantom Rei, ep19 train sequence dream Rei, and the possibility that Rei's eternal existance might in some way be the very reason that Seele knew 3I was inevitable or even why the FAR sent out instructions of what not to do with their stuff.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Wed Jan 29, 2014 7:39 pm We are explicitly shown that Rei's soul is still with us at the end of EOE: [wikimg width=400:i8i6hqhf]File:M26 C468 Rei III L.C.L Sea.jpg[/wikimg:i8i6hqhf]

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Wed Jan 29, 2014 8:23 pm ^This. As far as I'm concerned that's the end of it. If that isn't convincing enough for you also note that we are shown this, right after Rei's apparent death, at the same time we are also shown that Asuka and Shinji are, in fact, fine. Oh, and don't forget that it isn't just Rei, but the Kaworu x Rei god.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Jan 30, 2014 1:07 am NemZ wrote: I think she's stepped out of the linear experience of time and become a being coexistant with all places and times simultaneously. My evidence comes in the form of ep1 phantom Rei, ep19 train sequence dream Rei, and the possibility that Rei's eternal existance might in some way be the very reason that Seele knew 3I was inevitable or even why the FAR sent out instructions of what not to do with their stuff.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Jan 30, 2014 4:35 pm Sachi wrote: I'm rather intrigued by this theory, particularly that last bit regarding the FAR. Are there any prior threads you can link me to, or do you care to elaborate?

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Thu Jan 30, 2014 4:48 pm ^Which is quite interesting except that there is absolutely no suggestion that this is what occurred. Time travel or whatever you want to call it was never mentioned or implied in any way!

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Jan 30, 2014 5:25 pm yes, it is definately implied, as has been noted above. There are multiple examples in the series and especially in EoE that show Rei appearing in multiple places at once and at times BEFORE her asenction, showing that time and space are no longer hard bounderies to her existance.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Jan 30, 2014 6:42 pm Lavinius wrote: ^Which is quite interesting except that there is absolutely no suggestion that this is what occurred. Time travel or whatever you want to call it was never mentioned or implied in any way!

[]:             Quote Post by Gorbatschow » Fri Jan 31, 2014 1:19 am NemZ wrote: I don't know that I've stated it before, but basically my point is that if she has indeed become omnipresent then her very existance in prior historical moments

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Jan 31, 2014 1:54 am Most of the SEELE scenes involve Kiel telling Gendo that under no circumstances is he to make a god. That sort of suggests that the possibility is in his hands.

[]:             Quote Post by Gorbatschow » Fri Jan 31, 2014 2:37 am Mr. Tines wrote: Most of the SEELE scenes involve Kiel telling Gendo that under no circumstances is he to make a god. That sort of suggests that the possibility is in his hands.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Jan 31, 2014 11:24 am Gorbatschow wrote: Furthermore they use the word God quite inflationary, since they seemingly consider the EVAs gods.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Jan 31, 2014 1:12 pm Well, let's break this down. Post EoE Rei is: 1) able to be at any place or time in existence = omnipresence 2) a witness to events at all such times and places = omniscience 3) in possession of a rediculously powerful ATF, which can basically alter matter and energy however she pleases = omnipotence so... how is she not god again?

[]:             Quote Post by Gorbatschow » Fri Jan 31, 2014 1:42 pm NemZ wrote: Well, let's break this down. Post EoE Rei is: 1) able to be at any place or time in existence = omnipresence 2) a witness to events at all such times and places = omniscience 3) in possession of a rediculously powerful ATF, which can basically alter matter and energy however she pleases = omnipotence so... how is she not god again?

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Fri Jan 31, 2014 2:24 pm She's not the God but she's definitely a god.

[]:             Quote Post by Gorbatschow » Fri Jan 31, 2014 2:41 pm Chuckman wrote: She's not the God but she's definitely a god.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Fri Jan 31, 2014 2:56 pm She's absolutely a supernatural being, even if only by the sufficiently advanced technology version. "God" doesn't really have a strict definition. I know one when I see one. Rei is way more powerful than, say, Zeus.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Jan 31, 2014 3:00 pm NemZ wrote: Well, let's break this down. Post EoE Rei is: 1) able to be at any place or time in existence = omnipresence 2) a witness to events at all such times and places = omniscience 3) in possession of a rediculously powerful ATF, which can basically alter matter and energy however she pleases = omnipotence so... how is she not god again?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Jan 31, 2014 3:08 pm Rei is a god because no mortal would be able to tell the difference between her and another deity. There becomes a point where Sufficiently Superpowerful and Actually Omnipotent are impossible to distinguish, and she has reached that point.
